transcript: Hi lovely people of Foodtube! Look what I am! I am in my garden and I'm gonna cook a fantastic minestrone with 12 different vegetable insides. Remember you can make this minestrone any time of the year and let's enjoy. We just have to cook all this very very slowly. So peas, burrotti beans, broad beans, onion, so easy, so delicious. Celery, hallelujah, I love the celery, the lovely flavour of celery. Oh yes, yellow and green, look broccolilude the stalk of a broccoli. Come on, patito. Pumpkins. Straight inside. Carrots. Lovely. Cabbage. You know you need cabbage. Tomato. About ten, twenty. Basil. Just put everything inside inside and then you're gonna have a very very good stock so that is all boiling nice long season already you don't have to do anything you don't want to put all the peel stuff inside it let's put it back yeah let's lift this one look at that drizzle of some nice extra virgin olive oil then you crush the garlic but three cloves of garlic crush it it takes more longer to peel a garlic if they actually put everything together. You must use stalk of parsley. It's got so much flavour but you don't have to do fine. Ah, yes. What a flavour. You stir them a little bit. Look how delicious everything is inside. I want to get the tomato as well. Fantastic. Right, let's bring them up to a boil right now. It tastes so good. And when they come up to a boil, you put a nice paste inside. It looks like spaghetti, but actually it's got a little hole in the bucatini. Bucatini, they're very good. You can use also different shapes of pasta. I mean you've got some spaghetti left over, just, you know, break it. Handful. Don't put it too much. Just a handful. You stir it and you wait for the pasta to be cooked look at this everything is done now i'm gonna turn it off let's start to serve it which dish i'm gonna use it this one fantastic lovely and rustic we also oh look at that appropriate one of this one do you know what i love the three so let's do it so first one lovely minestrone lovely minestra not too much oh yes there is a little bit of basil on every single one because the basil gives a lovely flavor little parmesan drizzle of olive oil and this is chunky minestrone. If you want to see me make more of this fantastic dish, what are we going to do? Watch Foodtube channel or General Contaldo YouTube and let's enjoy it all together like now. For the moment arrivederci, I love you all and i've got quite a few people to feed here ciao this recipe was adapted from one in my box called gennaro is italian let me know what you think you lovely peoples love you all
